summary: Iter53 — Light/Dark theme toggle tested on public Home page. 5 of 6 checks PASS. Default is dark (ff_theme dataset='dark', reaper-bg rendered, Sun icon in toggle, CTA='Deal Your Fate!'). Clicking theme-toggle-button switches to light (cafe-bg-light rendered, reaper-bg absent, logo src='/logo-mark-light.png', CTA='Spin the Wheel', Moon icon). Persistence via localStorage 'ff_theme' works both directions across reloads. documentElement.dataset.ffTheme correctly reflects current theme. Rapid toggling (6x) produced ZERO console/page errors. Single ZIP=90210 spin in light mode: shuffle-popup opened with light overlay, 5 card-back elements each rendered (coffee/bagel back), skeleton-hands-overlay was ABSENT (as required in light mode), and a spin-result-card appeared. BUG: the reveal reaper-line in light mode still shows a DARK line ('Your fate is written.') — should use lightLineFor() (LIGHT_LINES: 'Your destination awaits.', 'Here's tonight's pick.', 'Your table is set.', 'A great choice, locked in.', 'Bon appétit — go enjoy.').

ui_bugs.issue: reaper-line always uses reaperLineFor(card); should switch to lightLineFor(card) when in light theme. Observed light-mode reveal displayed 'Your fate is written.' (a macabre line), not one of the professional LIGHT_LINES. lightLineFor is imported (line 25) but never called. Also the accompanying Skull icon and red #E01E26 color may want a lighter/cheerful treatment in light mode.

action_items: In frontend/src/pages/Home.jsx around line 1677, use lightLineFor(card) when the light theme is active (pass a `light` prop into RevealStage or read useTheme() there). Consider swapping the Skull icon / red accent for a lighter icon/color in light mode to match the softened cream editorial aesthetic.
critical_code_review_comments: lightLineFor is imported but unused — dead import unless wired into RevealStage.
useTheme hook is clean and correct (localStorage 'ff_theme', dataset.ffTheme, dark default).
Consider also theming the reveal-card typography color when light (currently text-[#E01E26] italic Skull row feels dark-mode-only).

success_rate.frontend: 5/6 checks passing (theme default, toggle, persistence, dataset, no-errors, shuffle assets ✓ / reveal-line in light ✗)

seed_data_creation: one Places search performed (ZIP 90210) — landed on 'Café Amici Beverly Hills'. Only ONE spin was executed, honoring the Google Places daily-quota constraint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Theme toggle mechanics (localStorage, dataset, header/background/logo/CTA-text/card-back-image swap, absence of skeleton overlay in light) are all verified. The only remaining issue is the reveal reaper-line in light mode — after fix, verify by toggling to light, entering ZIP 90210, clicking spin-roulette-button, waiting for spin-result-card, and asserting reaper-line innerText is one of LIGHT_LINES. Remember: HARD CAP of 160 Places calls/day; do at most ONE spin per test run.
rca of the issue: Home.jsx line 1677 unconditionally renders `reaperLineFor(card)`. The `light` boolean from useTheme() is not passed/used at the RevealStage detail block, so LIGHT_LINES (defined in homeConstants.js) are never rendered even though lightLineFor is exported and imported. Fix: pass `light` down to the reveal detail (or import useTheme locally) and switch to `light ? lightLineFor(card) : reaperLineFor(card)`.
